message:
fix(core): scope schema borrow during reload to avoid RefCell double-borrow

Wrap `schema_fingerprint(&self.schema.borrow())` inside
`reload_memdb_from_txn_with_mode` in an explicit block so the
`Ref<Vec<TableSchema>>` is definitively dropped before the subsequent
`*self.schema.borrow_mut() = new_schema` statement.

Relying on temporary-lifetime-ends-at-`;` proved insufficient in
practice: a reentrant drop of the old `MemDatabase` triggered by the
preceding `*self.db.borrow_mut() = new_db` assignment on an asupersync
blocking worker thread was observed to still hold the `Ref` when the
next `borrow_mut()` fired, producing

  thread 'asupersync-worker-blocking-blocking-4' panicked at
  fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs: RefCell already borrowed

The explicit inner scope removes the dependency on temporary-lifetime
heuristics and makes the drop point syntactically deterministic.

Verified via `cargo check -p fsqlite-core`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
